taskDescription: Complete the crossword on the Medulla Oblongata

words.clue: Rounded ridge on each side of the anterior median fissure containing corticospinal fibres
Oval eminence lateral to the pyramid overlying the inferior olivary nucleus
Lateral medullary syndrome is also known as this eponymous syndrome
Artery whose occlusion causes lateral medullary syndrome
Nucleus that receives fine touch and proprioception from the lower limb
Point at the lower end of the 4th ventricle floor marking the central canal opening
Cranial nerve whose rootlets emerge between pyramid and olive
Percentage of corticospinal fibres that cross at the pyramidal decussation

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
